task: Design a customer 360 data application for support agents.
assumptions: ticketing system is canonical for support events
support UI tolerates minutes of freshness lag
pattern: Use canonical operational owners, a warehouse/lakehouse analytical layer, materialized support-facing summaries, and reconciliation checks for stale or missing source feeds.
incompatibilities: hard real-time fraud decisions
single canonical write surface for all customer facts

task: Store all product knowledge and permissions only in embeddings for an AI assistant.
assumptions: assistant needs factual permissions and auditability
pattern: Do not use vector retrieval as the source of truth for governed facts; use canonical structured records plus derived retrieval indexes.

task: Model customers, employees, vendors, and account managers for an enterprise CRM.
assumptions: same real-world organization can play multiple roles
names and identifiers may have history
pattern: Use Party as the stable concept, Party Relationship/Role for customer/vendor/employee/manager participation, and separate name/identifier structures for history and issuing authority.
incompatibilities: single-purpose app where separate role tables are intentionally local and non-integrated

task: Build a canonical data model directly around legacy paper forms and report screens.
assumptions: forms summarize or transmit underlying business facts
pattern: Do not treat forms/screens as canonical entities by default; extract the underlying parties, assets, activities, locations, contracts, and information resources.
incompatibilities: the form itself is governed, approved, retained, transmitted, or audited as a business object

task: Design profile updates with read replicas for a global consumer app.
assumptions: users expect to see their own edits immediately
pattern: Route the writer's session to the leader or a sufficiently caught-up replica, expose follower lag metrics, and reserve stale follower reads for users who did not just write.
incompatibilities: multi-region concurrent edits without conflict semantics
hard global linearizability requirement

task: Keep a product search index up to date from an operational catalog database.
assumptions: database is canonical for product facts
search index may lag by seconds
pattern: Use CDC/outbox into a durable log, make index writes idempotent by product/version, monitor lag and dead letters, and retain a full rebuild path from the source of record.
incompatibilities: index is allowed to become canonical source
freshness must be strictly linearizable

task: Shard high-volume event ingestion by creation timestamp because dashboards read recent time ranges.
assumptions: writes arrive mostly at current time
pattern: Do not use a purely monotonic timestamp partition key for high write load; combine time bucketing with hashing/salting or separate write-optimized ingestion from time-range analytical layout.
incompatibilities: low write rate archive where simple time partitions are intentionally sufficient

task: Design a small internal expense tracker with simple approvals and reports.
pattern: Use presentation/domain/data-source separation, Transaction Script for use cases, and Table Data Gateway or Row Data Gateway to keep SQL out of presentation logic.
incompatibilities: rapidly growing special-case business rules
rich domain behavior shared by multiple clients

task: Design a leasing platform with complex pricing, billing, early returns, and rule exceptions.
assumptions: business rules are complex and volatile
relational schema may not match object model
pattern: Use a rich Domain Model, Data Mapper, Unit of Work, Identity Map, selective Lazy Load, and optimistic or pessimistic offline locks based on conflict cost.
incompatibilities: one-screen CRUD app where mapping complexity would dominate

task: Scale an enterprise application by putting Customer, Order, Product, and Invoice objects on separate remote servers.
assumptions: distribution is proposed for scalability rather than a required boundary
pattern: Do not distribute fine-grained domain objects; keep object collaboration local and scale by clustering, adding coarse-grained Remote Facades and DTOs only at unavoidable remote boundaries.
incompatibilities: explicit independent service boundary with coarse-grained API and separate ownership

task: Design an enterprise metadata repository that reconciles business glossary terms with schemas and lineage.
assumptions: technical schema catalog exists but business meaning is inconsistent
pattern: Model business concept separately from term/signifier, scope term usage by semantic community and context, map concepts and fact types to entity classes, attributes, domains, tables, and columns, and assign stewards for concept and implementation changes.
incompatibilities: single-team project where a table catalog is intentionally enough

task: Trace loan approval policies into system controls and data-quality monitoring.
assumptions: rules constrain application data and workflow
pattern: Link policy to actionable business rules, affected concepts/fact types, system constraints, database/application/workflow enforcement, exception handling, and production data-quality checks with owners and thresholds.
incompatibilities: advisory guidance with no enforcement or audit requirement

task: Build an enterprise metadata repository by importing only database tables and columns.
assumptions: schema names are inconsistent across systems
pattern: Do not treat physical schema inventory as the whole metadata truth; add business concepts, terms, semantic communities, fact types, rules, stewards, and explicit mappings to technical artifacts.
incompatibilities: temporary DBA inventory where business semantics are explicitly out of scope

task: Design the relational schema for a customer-facing auction workflow with sellers, listings, bids, and buyer history.
assumptions: future reporting can be served separately
pattern: Analyze operations and business rules first, model core ERD tables and one-to-many relationships, normalize through practical 3NF, enforce primary/foreign keys, index important foreign keys, and defer speculative alternate indexes until query patterns are known.
incompatibilities: analyst-facing warehouse where star schema is the primary need

task: Create a warehouse model for reporting on bids, listings, sellers, categories, locations, and time.
pattern: Keep OLTP normalized for operations, build a separate star schema with a clearly grained fact table, direct dimensions for time/location/category/seller/bidder, warehouse surrogate keys for cross-source identity, and materialized summaries for repeated aggregations.
incompatibilities: small internal app where a separate warehouse is unnecessary

task: Normalize every candidate key, optional field, and multi-use attribute into separate tables before developers build the app.
assumptions: queries must be understandable and fast
edge constraints can be enforced elsewhere
pattern: Do not pursue beyond-3NF purity by default; stop where integrity benefits are concrete, because excessive granularity increases join complexity, performance risk, and maintenance cost.
incompatibilities: regulated data capture where the deeper constraint prevents a known serious anomaly

task: Add an index for every possible filter and sort field in a new OLTP schema before the application exists.
pattern: Do not create speculative alternate indexes; index keys needed for integrity and known high-value queries, then measure production or representative workload before adding more.
incompatibilities: read-only reporting copy with stable query workload

task: Choose storage behavior for a write-heavy event ingestion store with occasional point lookups and range scans.
pattern: Favor LSM/SSTable-style storage only if compaction, tombstones, Bloom filters, read amplification, and disk headroom are explicitly monitored and sized.
incompatibilities: strict low-tail-latency reads where compaction spikes are unacceptable

task: Design an index for OLTP records that need point lookups and ordered range scans.
pattern: Use B-Tree/B+Tree-style indexing, watch page split/merge churn and fragmentation, and schedule vacuum/maintenance if updates/deletes are heavy.
incompatibilities: append-only ingestion where write amplification dominates all other concerns

task: Make payment processing exactly once by retrying network calls until they succeed.
pattern: Do not equate retries with exactly-once processing; use idempotency keys, deduplication, transactional state, and audit evidence to make repeated delivery safe.
incompatibilities: purely idempotent read operation with no side effects

task: Design an order platform that needs an enterprise concept model, service API, OLTP schema, warehouse, search index, and storage-performance guidance.
assumptions: orders and payments are durable operational facts
search and warehouse projections may lag
pattern: Use Enterprise Model Patterns for Order/Party/Product/Payment semantics, PoEAA for a local domain model plus coarse service boundary, Beginning Database Design for normalized OLTP tables and warehouse grain, DDIA for source-of-record vs derived search/warehouse dataflows, and Database Internals only for measured index/storage bottlenecks.
incompatibilities: single small CRUD tool where separate layers would be unnecessary
existing mandated product whose internals cannot affect design choices

task: Use the same database tables as the conceptual enterprise model, service DTOs, warehouse schema, search documents, and metadata repository.
assumptions: the system has multiple audiences and workloads
business meaning, API shape, reporting, and retrieval have different change pressures
pattern: Do not force one artifact to serve every layer; map between conceptual entities, application objects/DTOs, normalized source-of-record tables, derived warehouse/search shapes, and metadata/stewardship records.
incompatibilities: throwaway prototype with one audience and no governance requirement
